topic.what: Cryptanalysis of Sharafi-Daghigh Ring-LWE-based digital signature scheme showing it is fundamentally insecure
topic.how: Flaw-by-design attack exploiting lack of binding between error terms and secret key allowing signature forgery without secret key access
topic.why_matters: Breaks UF-CMA unforgeability - adversary forges signatures for arbitrary messages using only public key, no patch possible

characteristics.what_it_measures: Probability that forged signature passes verification without secret key
characteristics.evidence: 1000 trials with re-sampling on rare failures
characteristics.context: Adversary generates valid signatures without any oracle access

characteristics.what_itasures: Wall-clock time to compute forged signature
characteristics.evidence: Average wall-clock time over trials on Intel Core i7 laptop
characteristics.context: Practical efficient attack on standard laptop

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational steps relative to scheme operations
characteristics.context: Attacker uses identical computation as legitimate signer

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of signing oracle queries for attack
characteristics.evidence: Zero-query key-only attack proven in Theorem 1
characteristics.context: No oracle access needed - pure public-key attack

good_for: understanding structural vulnerabilities in lattice signatures
identifying flaw-by-design patterns in hash-and-sign schemes
teaching signature scheme security design pitfalls

novelty: First key-only forgery attack on Sharafi-Daghigh scheme
Proof that error terms not bound to secret key enables universal forgery
Formal UF-CMA insecurity proof for Ring-LWE signature paradigm
Demonstration that flaw generalizes to plain/module-LWE variants
